https://dvwa.csalab.appnmap_1_1772012723.374994lowOperational Security Issue - Tool Misconfiguration### [LOW] Operational Security Issue - Tool Misconfiguration The command syntax shows improper target formatting, indicating potential gaps in operational procedures or automation. - Wasted scanning time and resources - False sense of security if results aren\'t properly validated - Potential blind spots in security assessment coverage - May indicate inadequate quality control in security testing processes **Recommendation:** 1. Implement input validation in scanning automation 2. Add pre-flight checks to verify target reachability 3. Parse and sanitize URLs to extract hostnames before passing to nmap 4. Review scanning procedures and operator training 5. Implement result validation to catch zero-result scans --- ## SUMMARY: No Security Assessment Performed **Overall Security Posture: UNKNOWN** This scan produced **zero security findings** because the target was never actually scanned. The nmap tool rejected the target specification as invalid due to the inclusion of the `https://` protocol prefix. ### Critical Points: 1. **No vulnerabilities identified** - Not because the target is secure, but because no scanning occurred 2. **No services enumerated** - Cannot assess exposed attack surface 3. **No version information gathered** - Cannot evaluate known CVEs 4. **Complete assessment failure** - This output provides zero security value ### Required Actions: **IMMEDIATE:** - Re-execute the scan with corrected syntax: `nmap -sV -sC -T4 dvwa.csalab.app` - Verify target is reachable before scanning - Consider DNS resolution: `nslookup dvwa.csalab.app` or `dig dvwa.csalab.app` **FOLLOW-UP:** - If this is DVWA (Damn Vulnerable Web Application), expect to find intentional vulnerabilities - Perform comprehensive port scanning (not just default 1000 ports) - Include web application-specific scanning tools (nikto, dirb, etc.) - Conduct manual verification of any automated findings ### False Positive Assessment: **No false positives present** - There are no findings to evaluate. However, there is a **critical false negative**: the absence of results might be misinterpreted as \"no vulnerabilities found\" when in reality, no assessment occurred. --- ## Analyst Notes: This is a textbook example of \"garbage in, garbage out.\" The tool was given invalid input and produced no actionable intelligence. Any security report based solely on this output would be fundamentally flawed and potentially dangerous, as it would leave the target completely unassessed while appearing to have been scanned. **Confidence Level:** HIGH (that no scanning occurred) **Actionable Findings:** 0 **Requires Re-scan:** YES - CRITICAL PRIORITY **nmap2026-02-25T09:45:23.375011nmap_1772012695488<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?> <!DOCTYPE nmaprun> <?xml-stylesheet href=\"file:///usr/bin/../share/nmap/nmap.xsl\" type=\"text/xsl\"?> <!-- Nmap 7.97 scan initiated Wed Feb 25 09:44:51 2026 as: nmap -sV -sC -T4 -oX - https://dvwa.csalab.appnuclei_0_1772013242.423974criticalDefault Administrative Credentials**### **[CRITICAL] Default Administrative Credentials** - **Evidence**: Successful authentication using `admin:password` credentials on `/login.php`. HTTP 200 response with authenticated session showing full application menu including admin functions (Setup/Reset DB, vulnerability modules). - **Impact**: Complete compromise of the application. Attackers gain full administrative access to: - Database reset/setup functionality - All vulnerability testing modules (SQL Injection, Command Injection, RCE, etc.) - Potential to pivot to underlying infrastructure - Ability to modify application configuration - Access to any stored data or user information - **Recommendation**: - Immediately change default credentials to strong, unique passwords - Implement account lockout policies after failed login attempts - Enable multi-factor authentication (MFA) for administrative accounts - Review access logs for unauthorized access - Consider IP whitelisting for administrative access nuclei2026-02-25T09:54:02.423990nuclei_1772013209126{\"template\":\"http/default-logins/dvwa/dvwa-default-login.yaml\",\"template-url\":\"https://cloud.projectdiscovery.io/public/dvwa-default-login\",\"template-id\":\"dvwa-default-login\",\"template-path\":\"/home/guardian/nuclei-templates/http/default-logins/dvwa/dvwa-default-login.yaml\",\"info\":{\"name\":\"DVWA Default Login\",\"author\":[\"pdteam\"],\"tags\":[\"dvwa\",\"default-login\",\"vuln\"],\"description\":\"Damn Vulnerable Web App (DVWA) is a test application for security professionals. The hard coded credentials are part of a security testing scenario.\",\"reference\":[\"https://opensourcelibs.com/lib/dvwa\"],\"severity\":\"critical\",\"metadata\":{\"max-request\":2},\"classification\":{\"cve-id\":null,\"cwe-id\":[\"cwe-798\"]}},\"type\":\"http\",\"host\":\"dvwa.csalab.app\",\"port\":\"443\",\"scheme\":\"https\",\"url\":\"https://dvwa.csalab.app\",\"matched-at\":\"https://dvwa.csalab.app/index.php\",\"request\":\"POST /login.php HTTP/1.1\\r\\nHost: dvwa.csalab.app\\r\\nUser-Agent: Mozilla/5.0 (watchTowr; Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64; rv:84.0) Gecko/20100101 Firefox/84.0\\r\\nContent-Length: 88\\r\\nConnection: close\\r\\nContent-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded\\r\\nCookie: PHPSESSID=up22hf456i3d34nq04i6kgog2p; security=low; security=low; PHPSESSID=up22hf456i3d34nq04i6kgog2p\\r\\nAccept-Encoding: gzip\\r\\n\\r\\nusername=admin\\u0026password=password\\u0026Login=Login\\u0026user_token=0b44d9da717f6f5a0c19209658988ce7\",\"response\":\"HTTP/1.1 200 OK\\r\\nConnection: close\\r\\nTransfer-Encoding: chunked\\r\\nAlt-Svc: h3=\\\":443\\\"; ma=86400\\r\\nCache-Control: no-cache, must-revalidate\\r\\nCf-Cache-Status: DYNAMIC\\r\\nCf-Ray: 9d364e3439bb09ef-HKG\\r\\nContent-Type: text/html;charset=utf-8\\r\\nDate: Wed, 25 Feb 2026 09:51:40 GMT\\r\\nExpires: Tue, 23 Jun 2009 12:00:00 GMT\\r\\nNel: {\\\"report_to\\\":\\\"cf-nel\\\",\\\"success_fraction\\\":0.0,\\\"max_age\\\":604800}\\r\\nPragma: no-cache\\r\\nReport-To: {\\\"group\\\":\\\"cf-nel\\\",\\\"max_age\\\":604800,\\\"endpoints\\\":[{\\\"url\\\":\\\"https://a.nel.cloudflare.com/report/v4?s=acIhc4od6J2HZuLxMORj%2F5aUdIJ%2BKdY2HfoLn5q4Hh6oRINYLk3SEb%2F1X6uu4dLdfbgBFREMSSm8f05xl
https://dvwa.csalab.appnuclei_1_1772013242.424079highHardcoded Credentials (CWE-798)**### **[HIGH] Hardcoded Credentials (CWE-798)** - **Evidence**: Template classification indicates CWE-798 (Use of Hard-coded Credentials). The application ships with well-known default credentials that were not changed during deployment. - **Impact**: - Credentials are publicly documented and easily discoverable - Automated scanners can detect and exploit this vulnerability - No authentication barrier for attackers - Violates security best practices for credential management - **Recommendation**: - Remove all hardcoded credentials from application code - Implement forced password change on first login - Use environment variables or secure credential stores - Implement credential rotation policies nuclei2026-02-25T09:54:02.424087nuclei_1772013209126{\"template\":\"http/default-logins/dvwa/dvwa-default-login.yaml\",\"template-url\":\"https://cloud.projectdiscovery.io/public/dvwa-default-login\",\"template-id\":\"dvwa-default-login\",\"template-path\":\"/home/guardian/nuclei-templates/http/default-logins/dvwa/dvwa-default-login.yaml\",\"info\":{\"name\":\"DVWA Default Login\",\"author\":[\"pdteam\"],\"tags\":[\"dvwa\",\"default-login\",\"vuln\"],\"description\":\"Damn Vulnerable Web App (DVWA) is a test application for security professionals. The hard coded credentials are part of a security testing scenario.\",\"reference\":[\"https://opensourcelibs.com/lib/dvwa\"],\"severity\":\"critical\",\"metadata\":{\"max-request\":2},\"classification\":{\"cve-id\":null,\"cwe-id\":[\"cwe-798\"]}},\"type\":\"http\",\"host\":\"dvwa.csalab.app\",\"port\":\"443\",\"scheme\":\"https\",\"url\":\"https://dvwa.csalab.app\",\"matched-at\":\"https://dvwa.csalab.app/index.php\",\"request\":\"POST /login.php HTTP/1.1\\r\\nHost: dvwa.csalab.app\\r\\nUser-Agent: Mozilla/5.0 (watchTowr; Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64; rv:84.0) Gecko/20100101 Firefox/84.0\\r\\nContent-Length: 88\\r\\nConnection: close\\r\\nContent-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded\\r\\nCookie: PHPSESSID=up22hf456i3d34nq04i6kgog2p; security=low; security=low; PHPSESSID=up22hf456i3d34nq04i6kgog2p\\r\\nAccept-Encoding: gzip\\r\\n\\r\\nusername=admin\\u0026password=password\\u0026Login=Login\\u0026user_token=0b44d9da717f6f5a0c19209658988ce7\",\"response\":\"HTTP/1.1 200 OK\\r\\nConnection: close\\r\\nTransfer-Encoding: chunked\\r\\nAlt-Svc: h3=\\\":443\\\"; ma=86400\\r\\nCache-Control: no-cache, must-revalidate\\r\\nCf-Cache-Status: DYNAMIC\\r\\nCf-Ray: 9d364e3439bb09ef-HKG\\r\\nContent-Type: text/html;charset=utf-8\\r\\nDate: Wed, 25 Feb 2026 09:51:40 GMT\\r\\nExpires: Tue, 23 Jun 2009 12:00:00 GMT\\r\\nNel: {\\\"report_to\\\":\\\"cf-nel\\\",\\\"success_fraction\\\":0.0,\\\"max_age\\\":604800}\\r\\nPragma: no-cache\\r\\nReport-To: {\\\"group\\\":\\\"cf-nel\\\",\\\"max_age\\\":604800,\\\"endpoints\\\":[{\\\"url\\\":\\\"https://a.nel.cloudflare.com/report/v4?s=acIhc4od6J2HZuLxMORj%2F5aUdIJ%2BKdY2HfoLn5q4Hh6oRINYLk3SEb%2F1X6uu4dLdfbgBFREMSSm8f05xl
https://dvwa.csalab.appnuclei_2_1772013242.424162mediumPublicly Accessible Intentionally Vulnerable Application**### **[MEDIUM] Publicly Accessible Intentionally Vulnerable Application** - **Evidence**: DVWA instance accessible at `dvwa.csalab.app` on port 443 (HTTPS). Application contains multiple intentional vulnerabilities including SQL Injection, Command Injection, RCE, SSTI, Deserialization flaws. - **Impact**: - If this is a production environment, it presents extreme risk - Attackers can use this as a foothold to attack internal networks - May violate compliance requirements (PCI-DSS, HIPAA, etc.) - Reputation damage if compromised - **Recommendation**: - If this is a training environment: Isolate on separate network segment with no production access - Implement network segmentation and firewall rules - Restrict access to authorized users only (VPN, IP whitelisting) - If unintentional deployment: Remove immediately - Add authentication layer before DVWA application nuclei2026-02-25T09:54:02.424169nuclei_1772013209126{\"template\":\"http/default-logins/dvwa/dvwa-default-login.yaml\",\"template-url\":\"https://cloud.projectdiscovery.io/public/dvwa-default-login\",\"template-id\":\"dvwa-default-login\",\"template-path\":\"/home/guardian/nuclei-templates/http/default-logins/dvwa/dvwa-default-login.yaml\",\"info\":{\"name\":\"DVWA Default Login\",\"author\":[\"pdteam\"],\"tags\":[\"dvwa\",\"default-login\",\"vuln\"],\"description\":\"Damn Vulnerable Web App (DVWA) is a test application for security professionals. The hard coded credentials are part of a security testing scenario.\",\"reference\":[\"https://opensourcelibs.com/lib/dvwa\"],\"severity\":\"critical\",\"metadata\":{\"max-request\":2},\"classification\":{\"cve-id\":null,\"cwe-id\":[\"cwe-798\"]}},\"type\":\"http\",\"host\":\"dvwa.csalab.app\",\"port\":\"443\",\"scheme\":\"https\",\"url\":\"https://dvwa.csalab.app\",\"matched-at\":\"https://dvwa.csalab.app/index.php\",\"request\":\"POST /login.php HTTP/1.1\\r\\nHost: dvwa.csalab.app\\r\\nUser-Agent: Mozilla/5.0 (watchTowr; Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64; rv:84.0) Gecko/20100101 Firefox/84.0\\r\\nContent-Length: 88\\r\\nConnection: close\\r\\nContent-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded\\r\\nCookie: PHPSESSID=up22hf456i3d34nq04i6kgog2p; security=low; security=low; PHPSESSID=up22hf456i3d34nq04i6kgog2p\\r\\nAccept-Encoding: gzip\\r\\n\\r\\nusername=admin\\u0026password=password\\u0026Login=Login\\u0026user_token=0b44d9da717f6f5a0c19209658988ce7\",\"response\":\"HTTP/1.1 200 OK\\r\\nConnection: close\\r\\nTransfer-Encoding: chunked\\r\\nAlt-Svc: h3=\\\":443\\\"; ma=86400\\r\\nCache-Control: no-cache, must-revalidate\\r\\nCf-Cache-Status: DYNAMIC\\r\\nCf-Ray: 9d364e3439bb09ef-HKG\\r\\nContent-Type: text/html;charset=utf-8\\r\\nDate: Wed, 25 Feb 2026 09:51:40 GMT\\r\\nExpires: Tue, 23 Jun 2009 12:00:00 GMT\\r\\nNel: {\\\"report_to\\\":\\\"cf-nel\\\",\\\"success_fraction\\\":0.0,\\\"max_age\\\":604800}\\r\\nPragma: no-cache\\r\\nReport-To: {\\\"group\\\":\\\"cf-nel\\\",\\\"max_age\\\":604800,\\\"endpoints\\\":[{\\\"url\\\":\\\"https://a.nel.cloudflare.com/report/v4?s=acIhc4od6J2HZuLxMORj%2F5aUdIJ%2BKdY2HfoLn5q4Hh6oRINYLk3SEb%2F1X6uu4dLdfbgBFREMSSm8f05xl
https://dvwa.csalab.appnuclei_3_1772013242.424239lowSecurity Headers Present**### **[LOW] Security Headers Present** - **Evidence**: Response includes security headers: - `Strict-Transport-Security: max-age=15552000; includeSubDomains; preload` - `X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff` - CloudFlare protection enabled - **Impact**: Positive finding - some security controls are in place at the infrastructure level - **Recommendation**: Continue maintaining security headers; consider adding: - Content-Security-Policy (CSP) - X-Frame-Options - Referrer-Policy --- ## FALSE POSITIVE ASSESSMENT: **This is NOT a false positive.** The finding is confirmed: - ✅ Successful authentication demonstrated - ✅ Default credentials accepted - ✅ Authenticated response received with privileged menu items - ✅ HTTP 200 status confirms successful login - ✅ Session cookies set (PHPSESSID) The vulnerability is real and exploitable. --- ## POTENTIAL ATTACK VECTORS: 1. **Direct Exploitation**: Immediate admin access via default credentials 2. **Privilege Escalation**: Use admin access to exploit intentional vulnerabilities (RCE, Command Injection) 3. **Data Exfiltration**: Access to database through SQL Injection modules or direct DB access 4. **Lateral Movement**: Compromise underlying server and pivot to internal network 5. **Denial of Service**: Use \"Setup/Reset DB\" functionality to disrupt service 6. **Malware Hosting**: Upload malicious files through vulnerable modules --- ## SUMMARY: **Overall Security Posture: CRITICAL RISK** The target application has **critical security vulnerabilities** that allow immediate, unauthenticated administrative access. The presence of default credentials (`admin:password`) on a publicly accessible DVWA instance represents a complete security failure. **Key Concerns:** - Zero authentication barrier to full administrative control - Intentionally vulnerable application exposed to public internet - Multiple high-risk vulnerability modules accessible post-authentication - Potential for complete system compromise and lateral movement **Immediate Actions Required:** 1. Change default credentials immediately 2. Assess if this is an authorized deployment 3. Implement network-level access controls 4. Review logs for evidence of compromise 5. Consider taking system offline until secured **Risk Rating**: This finding alone warrants a **CRITICAL** security rating for the entire application. The combination of default credentials and intentionally vulnerable software creates an extremely high-risk scenario that requires immediate remediation. nuclei2026-02-25T09:54:02.424244nuclei_1772013209126{\"template\":\"http/default-logins/dvwa/dvwa-default-login.yaml\",\"template-url\":\"https://cloud.projectdiscovery.io/public/dvwa-default-login\",\"template-id\":\"dvwa-default-login\",\"template-path\":\"/home/guardian/nuclei-templates/http/default-logins/dvwa/dvwa-default-login.yaml\",\"info\":{\"name\":\"DVWA Default Login\",\"author\":[\"pdteam\"],\"tags\":[\"dvwa\",\"default-login\",\"vuln\"],\"description\":\"Damn Vulnerable Web App (DVWA) is a test application for security professionals. The hard coded credentials are part of a security testing scenario.\",\"reference\":[\"https://opensourcelibs.com/lib/dvwa\"],\"severity\":\"critical\",\"metadata\":{\"max-request\":2},\"classification\":{\"cve-id\":null,\"cwe-id\":[\"cwe-798\"]}},\"type\":\"http\",\"host\":\"dvwa.csalab.app\",\"port\":\"443\",\"scheme\":\"https\",\"url\":\"https://dvwa.csalab.app\",\"matched-at\":\"https://dvwa.csalab.app/index.php\",\"request\":\"POST /login.php HTTP/1.1\\r\\nHost: dvwa.csalab.app\\r\\nUser-Agent: Mozilla/5.0 (watchTowr; Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64; rv:84.0) Gecko/20100101 Firefox/84.0\\r\\nContent-Length: 88\\r\\nConnection: close\\r\\nContent-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded\\r\\nCookie: PHPSESSID=up22hf456i3d34nq04i6kgog2p; security=low; security=low; PHPSESSID=up22hf456i3d34nq04i6kgog2p\\r\\nAccept-Encoding: gzip\\r\\n\\r\\nusername=admin\\u0026password=password\\u0026Login=Login\\u0026user_token=0b44d9da717f6f5a0c19209658988ce7\",\"response\":\"HTTP/1.1 200 OK\\r\\nConnection: close\\r\\nTransfer-Encoding: chunked\\r\\nAlt-Svc: h3=\\\":443\\\"; ma=86400\\r\\nCache-Control: no-cache, must-revalidate\\r\\nCf-Cache-Status: DYNAMIC\\r\\nCf-Ray: 9d364e3439bb09ef-HKG\\r\\nContent-Type: text/html;charset=utf-8\\r\\nDate: Wed, 25 Feb 2026 09:51:40 GMT\\r\\nExpires: Tue, 23 Jun 2009 12:00:00 GMT\\r\\nNel: {\\\"report_to\\\":\\\"cf-nel\\\",\\\"success_fraction\\\":0.0,\\\"max_age\\\":604800}\\r\\nPragma: no-cache\\r\\nReport-To: {\\\"group\\\":\\\"cf-nel\\\",\\\"max_age\\\":604800,\\\"endpoints\\\":[{\\\"url\\\":\\\"https://a.nel.cloudflare.com/report/v4?s=acIhc4od6J2HZuLxMORj%2F5aUdIJ%2BKdY2HfoLn5q4Hh6oRINYLk3SEb%2F1X6uu4dLdfbgBFREMSSm8f05xl
https://dvwa.csalab.appwhatweb_0_1772013310.66838criticalIntentionally Vulnerable Application Exposed to Internet**### **[CRITICAL] Intentionally Vulnerable Application Exposed to Internet** - **Evidence**: Title reveals \"Damn Vulnerable Web Application (DVWA)\" - **Impact**: DVWA contains multiple intentional vulnerabilities including SQL injection, XSS, CSRF, command injection, file inclusion, and more. If this is a production environment or accessible to unauthorized users, it represents an immediate and severe security risk. Attackers can exploit these vulnerabilities to: - Gain unauthorized access to the system - Execute arbitrary commands on the server - Steal sensitive data - Pivot to internal network resources - **Recommendation**: - **IMMEDIATE**: Verify this is in an isolated training/lab environment - Restrict access via IP whitelisting or VPN - Never expose DVWA to the public internet - If this is unintentional, take the application offline immediately - Implement network segmentation to isolate from production systems whatweb2026-02-25T09:55:10.668396whatweb_1772013270409[1m[34mhttps://dvwa.csalab.app[0m [200 OK] [1mCookies[0m[[0m[22mPHPSESSID,security[0m], [1mCountry[0m[[0m[22mUNITED STATES[0m][[1m[31mUS[0m], [1mHTML5[0m, [1mHTTPServer[0m[[1m[36mcloudflare[0m], [1mIP[0m[[0m[22m104.21.28.88[0m], [1mScript[0m[[0m[22mtext/javascript[0m], [1mStrict-Transport-Security[0m[[0m[22mmax-age=15552000; includeSubDomains; preload[0m], [1mTitle[0m[[1m[33mWelcome :: Damn Vulnerable Web Application (DVWA)[0m], [1mUncommonHeaders[0m[[0m[22mnel,report-to,cf-cache-status,x-content-type-options,cf-ray,alt-svc[0m]
https://dvwa.csalab.appwhatweb_1_1772013310.668479highPHP Session Cookie Without Secure Flag Verification**### **[HIGH] PHP Session Cookie Without Secure Flag Verification** - **Evidence**: Cookie \"PHPSESSID\" detected - **Impact**: While HSTS is present (forcing HTTPS), we cannot confirm from WhatWeb output alone whether the PHPSESSID cookie has the Secure and HttpOnly flags set. Missing flags could allow: - Session hijacking via XSS attacks - Session interception if HTTPS is bypassed - **Recommendation**: - Verify PHPSESSID cookie has Secure and HttpOnly flags - Implement SameSite=Strict attribute to prevent CSRF - Use session regeneration after authentication whatweb2026-02-25T09:55:10.668487whatweb_1772013270409[1m[34mhttps://dvwa.csalab.app[0m [200 OK] [1mCookies[0m[[0m[22mPHPSESSID,security[0m], [1mCountry[0m[[0m[22mUNITED STATES[0m][[1m[31mUS[0m], [1mHTML5[0m, [1mHTTPServer[0m[[1m[36mcloudflare[0m], [1mIP[0m[[0m[22m104.21.28.88[0m], [1mScript[0m[[0m[22mtext/javascript[0m], [1mStrict-Transport-Security[0m[[0m[22mmax-age=15552000; includeSubDomains; preload[0m], [1mTitle[0m[[1m[33mWelcome :: Damn Vulnerable Web Application (DVWA)[0m], [1mUncommonHeaders[0m[[0m[22mnel,report-to,cf-cache-status,x-content-type-options,cf-ray,alt-svc[0m]
https://dvwa.csalab.appwhatweb_2_1772013310.668542mediumCustom Security Cookie Detected**### **[MEDIUM] Custom Security Cookie Detected** - **Evidence**: Cookie named \"security\" present - **Impact**: DVWA uses this cookie to set difficulty levels (low, medium, high, impossible). This could: - Allow attackers to manipulate security settings - Bypass security controls by changing cookie values - Reveal application logic and security mechanisms - **Recommendation**: - Security levels should be server-side controlled - Implement proper session-based authorization - Never trust client-side security controls whatweb2026-02-25T09:55:10.668550whatweb_1772013270409[1m[34mhttps://dvwa.csalab.app[0m [200 OK] [1mCookies[0m[[0m[22mPHPSESSID,security[0m], [1mCountry[0m[[0m[22mUNITED STATES[0m][[1m[31mUS[0m], [1mHTML5[0m, [1mHTTPServer[0m[[1m[36mcloudflare[0m], [1mIP[0m[[0m[22m104.21.28.88[0m], [1mScript[0m[[0m[22mtext/javascript[0m], [1mStrict-Transport-Security[0m[[0m[22mmax-age=15552000; includeSubDomains; preload[0m], [1mTitle[0m[[1m[33mWelcome :: Damn Vulnerable Web Application (DVWA)[0m], [1mUncommonHeaders[0m[[0m[22mnel,report-to,cf-cache-status,x-content-type-options,cf-ray,alt-svc[0m]
https://dvwa.csalab.appwhatweb_3_1772013310.668701infoTechnology Stack Disclosure**### **[INFO] Technology Stack Disclosure** - **Evidence**: PHPSESSID cookie reveals PHP backend, HTML5 detected - **Impact**: Technology fingerprinting aids attackers in: - Selecting appropriate exploits - Identifying known vulnerabilities in PHP versions - Crafting targeted attacks - **Recommendation**: - Minimize technology disclosure where possible - Keep PHP version updated and patched - Consider using generic session names instead of PHPSESSID ### **[POSITIVE] HSTS Header Properly Configured** - **Evidence**: `Strict-Transport-Security: max-age=15552000; includeSubDomains; preload` - **Impact**: This is a **security positive**. HSTS forces browsers to use HTTPS, preventing downgrade attacks and SSL stripping - **Recommendation**: Maintain this configuration; consider HSTS preload list submission if appropriate ### **[POSITIVE] X-Content-Type-Options Header Present** - **Evidence**: Listed in UncommonHeaders - **Impact**: This header prevents MIME-type sniffing attacks, a **security positive** - **Recommendation**: Maintain this configuration whatweb2026-02-25T09:55:10.668714whatweb_1772013270409[1m[34mhttps://dvwa.csalab.app[0m [200 OK] [1mCookies[0m[[0m[22mPHPSESSID,security[0m], [1mCountry[0m[[0m[22mUNITED STATES[0m][[1m[31mUS[0m], [1mHTML5[0m, [1mHTTPServer[0m[[1m[36mcloudflare[0m], [1mIP[0m[[0m[22m104.21.28.88[0m], [1mScript[0m[[0m[22mtext/javascript[0m], [1mStrict-Transport-Security[0m[[0m[22mmax-age=15552000; includeSubDomains; preload[0m], [1mTitle[0m[[1m[33mWelcome :: Damn Vulnerable Web Application (DVWA)[0m], [1mUncommonHeaders[0m[[0m[22mnel,report-to,cf-cache-status,x-content-type-options,cf-ray,alt-svc[0m]
https://dvwa.csalab.appwhatweb_4_1772013310.668867infoCloudflare CDN/WAF in Use**### **[INFO] Cloudflare CDN/WAF in Use** - **Evidence**: HTTPServer shows cloudflare, CF-specific headers present - **Impact**: Cloudflare provides: - DDoS protection - Potential WAF capabilities (if enabled) - IP obfuscation (real server IP hidden) - However, Cloudflare alone cannot protect against application-layer vulnerabilities in DVWA - **Recommendation**: - Ensure Cloudflare WAF rules are enabled - Configure rate limiting - Do not rely solely on Cloudflare for security --- ## FALSE POSITIVE ASSESSMENT: **No false positives identified.** All findings are based on direct evidence from the tool output. The most critical finding (DVWA exposure) is confirmed by the application title in the HTTP response. --- ## POTENTIAL ATTACK VECTORS: Given this is DVWA, the following attack vectors are **intentionally present**: 1. **SQL Injection** - Database manipulation and data exfiltration 2. **Cross-Site Scripting (XSS)** - Session hijacking, credential theft 3. **Command Injection** - Remote code execution 4. **File Inclusion (LFI/RFI)** - Arbitrary file access, code execution 5. **CSRF** - Unauthorized actions on behalf of authenticated users 6. **Weak Session Management** - Session prediction/hijacking 7. **Insecure File Upload** - Malicious file upload leading to RCE 8. **Security Misconfiguration** - Exploitable default settings --- ## SUMMARY: Overall Security Posture **SECURITY POSTURE: CRITICAL RISK** This target is running DVWA (Damn Vulnerable Web Application), which is **intentionally insecure** and designed for penetration testing training. The security posture is **extremely poor by design**. **Key Concerns:** - ✗ Application contains multiple critical vulnerabilities by design - ✗ Exposure to internet (csalab.app domain suggests lab environment, but needs verification) - ✓ HSTS properly configured (positive) - ✓ Cloudflare protection layer present (positive, but insufficient) - ? Session cookie security flags unknown (requires further testing) **Critical Question**: Is this system supposed to be publicly accessible? **If YES (training/lab environment)**: - Ensure strict access controls (IP whitelisting, authentication) - Network isolation from production systems - Clear warnings for users - Monitoring for unauthorized access attempts **If NO (misconfiguration)**: - **IMMEDIATE ACTION REQUIRED**: Take offline or restrict access - Conduct incident response to determine if compromise occurred - Review how this was deployed to prevent recurrence **Next Steps for Comprehensive Assessment:** 1. Verify deployment intent and authorization 2. Test actual exploitability of DVWA vulnerabilities 3. Check cookie security attributes (Secure, HttpOnly, SameSite) 4. Assess network segmentation and access controls 5. Review Cloudflare WAF configuration and logs 6. Scan for additional exposed services on the server whatweb2026-02-25T09:55:10.668877whatweb_1772013270409[1m[34mhttps://dvwa.csalab.app[0m [200 OK] [1mCookies[0m[[0m[22mPHPSESSID,security[0m], [1mCountry[0m[[0m[22mUNITED STATES[0m][[1m[31mUS[0m], [1mHTML5[0m, [1mHTTPServer[0m[[1m[36mcloudflare[0m], [1mIP[0m[[0m[22m104.21.28.88[0m], [1mScript[0m[[0m[22mtext/javascript[0m], [1mStrict-Transport-Security[0m[[0m[22mmax-age=15552000; includeSubDomains; preload[0m], [1mTitle[0m[[1m[33mWelcome :: Damn Vulnerable Web Application (DVWA)[0m], [1mUncommonHeaders[0m[[0m[22mnel,report-to,cf-cache-status,x-content-type-options,cf-ray,alt-svc[0m]
